title: Battery
text: The battery is the heart of the electrical system. It stores energy as chemical potential and provides 12.6V DC when the engine is off. It also acts as a voltage stabilizer when the engine is running.

title: Alternator
text: The alternator generates AC power from engine rotation, then converts it to DC via internal diodes. It outputs 13.5-14.8V to charge the battery and power all electrical systems while the engine runs.

title: Fusible Link
text: This high-current protection device protects the alternator output circuit. Unlike regular fuses, fusible links are designed for very high current applications and are typically 4.0mm gauge wire.

title: Charge Warning Lamp
text: This dashboard indicator lights when the charging system isn't working. It completes the alternator's field circuit - if it stays on while driving, the alternator may have failed.

title: Engine Control Module
text: The ECM monitors and controls alternator output via the S (sense) terminal. It can adjust field current for optimal charging based on battery state and electrical load.

title: Engine Ground
text: Ground point E1 provides the return path for the charging circuit. A bad ground here can cause charging problems, dim lights, and erratic ECM behavior. Always check ground connections first!
